discussion_comments.comment_body: **issue(potentially-blocking)**: We've removed the `evaluate` call, which makes me a bit nervous. Previously, setting the value to `"false"` instead of `false` would work. Should we maybe add it back inside the `getEffectiveHtmlLabels` function? E.g. try out: ```mermaid-example --- config: flowchart: htmlLabels: "false" --- flowchart TD A -- Hi --> B ``` ```mermaid --- config: flowchart: htmlLabels: "false" --- flowchart TD A -- Hi --> B ```

**issue(potentially-blocking)**:

This seems to be a breaking change to me (although it's only a minor visual change, so maybe it's not actually breaking?):

Try rendering the following diagram in mermaid.live or on GitHub, and you can see the font is different for the axis labels:

```mermaid
---
config:
    themeVariables:
        fontFamily: 'Impact'
        textColor: 'red'
---
gantt
    title A Gantt Diagram
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    section Section
    A task           :a1, 2014-01-01, 1d
```

Your PR seems to allow changing the other vars, but not this one!

It looks like 50% of the CSS in Mermaid is using `var(--mermaid-font-family)` (aka `MermaidConfig["fontFamily"]`, but the other half seems to be using `options.fontFamily` (aka `MermaidConfig["themeVariables"]["fontFamily"]` and https://mermaid.js.org/config/theming.html#theme-variables

```suggestion
```
Consider making this configurable or derived from themeVariables
Hard-coding these values could cause issues if font size is different.
Why divided by 5? Can the icon size be configurable?
